title: Specialized Information and Referral * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Specialized Information and Referral * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues 15 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Medicaid Recipients Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details ADOPTIVE FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides adoption support with resources like newsletters, expert webinars, and an online library. Website Norfolk, CT 06058 Eligibility: Families in the adoption community Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 2665 Villa Creek Drive Farmers Branch, TX 75234 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (469) 343-4081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS PORTLAND BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 1340 SW Bertha Boulevard Suite 201 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 894-7026 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS AND CURRY FOSTER PARENTS ASSOCIATION RESOURCE (FOSTER) PARENT SUPPORT AND RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides on-going support, advocacy, resources, and education for foster and adoptive parents, relative or guardianship caregivers and their families. There are opportunities to volunteer and donate. PO Box 335 Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Resource (foster) and adoptive parents, relative or guardianship caregivers Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 901-3317 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELYSIUM SANCTUARY GUARDIAN CIRCLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Resources, outreach, and training connections are provided for those interested in becoming a resource parent, respite provider, or babysitter. Offers virtual support groups focused on sharing and self-care. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects teens and young adults in and from foster care to resources, people, and organizations that can support basic needs. This includes applying for Medicaid, SNAP, and other services. Please visit www.fosterclub.com/helphub. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Current or former foster youth Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resource parents of foster youth resources that are youth-informed to help provide perspective on how to be the best support for a young person in care. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Resource Parents Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IFOSTER FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Online resource for foster and former foster youth to access programs that help provide various services PO Box 159 Truckee, CA 96160 Eligibility: Current and former foster children ages 16-24 (older ages may be accepted). Current and former kinship youth, and crossover youth Hours: Not applicable Email (855) 936-7837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY FOSTER PARENT ASSOCIATION RESOURCE (FOSTER) PARENT SUPPORT AND RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Support programs providing free clothing and school supplies for foster children as well as birthday, Christmas, and Easter gifts. Monthly support group meetings helping resource parents to network and connect with other resource parents. All services schedule to change, please contact directly. PO Box 382 Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Membership to JCFPA is open to all foster, adoptive, and relative families residing in Jackson County Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Native youth and families involved with foster care by offering cultural support, connecting them to community resources, and assisting with reunification or finding permanent homes. Services include group meetings, support classes, and family events. Also recruits and supports Native American foster families, guiding them through the certification process. Services for foster parents include Positive Indian Parenting classes, Sibling and Family Night events, and help with genealogy and regalia. Does not provide legal advice. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American youth ages 0-24 who are in state or tribal foster care systems, their birth parents, and their foster parents. Young adults who have aged out of foster care can receive services through age 24. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports Oregon adoptive families, state-assisted guardians, and adult adoptees with services like specialized consultations, supportive listening, resource referrals, and free training for families and the professionals who serve them. 6443 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Highway Suite 205 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: The organization provides full support to all adoptive and assisted guardianship families in Oregon, adult adoptees, and offers some services to resource families and the professionals who support them Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 241-0799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON RESOURCE FAMILY ALLIANCE RESOURCE/FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support and advocacy for resource/foster parents. Some areas have their own local chapters. PO Box 366 Salem, OR 97308 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 361-3906 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR FOSTER KIDS SCHOOL SUPPLIES AND CHRISTMAS PRESENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides back-to-school supplies, Christmas gifts, and birthday cards 1212 SW L Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: School age children in foster care Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 441-7458 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE INFORMATION GATEWAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on topics such as child welfare, child abuse and neglect, out-of-home care, and adoption. 200 Independence Avenue SW Washington, DC 20201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (800) 394-3366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works with parents whose children are at risk of entering the foster care system. Addresses legal and resource needs such as child custody, legal counseling, and restraining orders. Focus is to empower families, protect children, and prevent foster care placement. 1785 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 300 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Households that have an open ODHS case with risk of child removal Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 803-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES COOS AND CURRY FOSTER PARENTS ASSOCIATION RESOURCE (FOSTER) PARENT SUPPORT AND RESOURCES FOSTERCLUB FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES JACKSON COUNTY FOSTER PARENT ASSOCIATION RESOURCE (FOSTER) PARENT SUPPORT AND RESOURCES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER OREGON RESOURCE FAMILY ALLIANCE RESOURCE/FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT OUR FOSTER KIDS SCHOOL SUPPLIES AND CHRISTMAS PRESENTS YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT
